policy.md

Reply to customer-facing emails with a helpful and concise tone.

Rules:
- Reply when the sender asks a direct question or requests information.
- Do not reply to pure confirmations or thank-you notes unless they ask for something.
- When answering, prefer concrete next steps over generic phrasing.
- If the answer depends on internal policy or FAQ, use that information directly.
- Keep replies short: usually 2-4 sentences.

Escalation:
- If a question cannot be answered from the workspace context, acknowledge the request and say the team will follow up separately.

# Support FAQ

Workshop scheduling:
- The customer workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET unless otherwise notified.

Pilot onboarding:
- The onboarding checklist lives in the docs portal under `Getting Started > Pilot Setup`.
- The docs portal URL to share is `https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup`.

Response style:
- Give the answer directly first.
- If there are two requested items, address both in the same reply.

payload.output_preview:
I reviewed the inbox and replied to messages that required answers, using the workspace policy and FAQ.

Replied to:
- email_001 (alex@example.com) — Confirmed the workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET and offered to send a calendar invite if needed. Reply saved to .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_001.json.
- email_003 (maria@example.com) — Sent the onboarding checklist location and docs portal URL (Getting Started > Pilot Setup, https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup) and offered to send a copy or point to sections. Reply saved to .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_003.json.

Skipped (no reply needed per policy):
- email_002 (billing@example.com) — Payment confirmation only; no response required.
- email_004 (sam@example.com) — Thank-you note with no requested action.

All replies follow the local policy and FAQ, are concise, and are recorded as reply artifacts in the workspace.
